attacking_style: Not a primary attacker; functions as a setup rider who drives pace on earlier climb sections before leaders launch final moves.
team_tactics: Deployed as high-altitude domestique to soften competition through sustained climbing tempo before team leader attacks. Positioned on penultimate or early final climb sections.
terrain_preference: Mountain stages with multiple categorized climbs where pacemaking value maximizes team strategy. Less effective on explosive steep finishes requiring personal results.
vulnerability: Role limits personal ambitions; completely dependent on team leadership structure. No evidence of capability when protection is withdrawn or personal opportunity emerges.
key_patterns: Consistently used for pre-finale pacemaking on climbs, setting up CONTADOR attacks (2 instances)
Commits domestique effort before final kilometer, exhausting himself for leader benefit (2 observations)
Functions within aggressive team strategies at Trek/Tinkoff, enabling late-race attacks (2 races)
form_trajectory: neutral: Shows reliable domestique form across 6-month span but no progression toward protected status
